title: parthenium argentatum f. marioloides
intro: Parthenium argentatum f. marioloides, also known as Mariola Sage or Mariola Thyme, is a variant of the Parthenium argentatum plant, a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Mariola Sage include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and provide relief from headaches and digestive issues. Mariola Sage can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
